resolutions.keep_english: A collection of small bubbles created by mixing soap with water.
Something, such as a monument, by which someone or something is remembered. [from 14th c.]
A terrifying and dangerous creature, especially one of an imaginary or mythical kind.
A clear deep blue variety of corundum, valued as a precious stone.
(firearms) A finger-operated lever used to fire a gun.
One who is harmed or killed by a crime or scam.
A particular manifestation of moral excellence in a person. [from 13th c.]
A device for causing acceleration.
One who acts for, or in the place of, another (the principal), by that person's authority; someone entrusted to act on behalf of or in behalf of another, such as to transact business for them.
A book specially designed to keep photographs, stamps, or autographs.
Of, or relating to any form of analysis, or to analytics.
An incorporeal and holy or semidivine messenger from a deity or other divine entity, traditionally depicted as a youthful, winged figure in flowing robes.
A flag or standard used by a military commander, monarch or nation.
(intransitive, followed by to) To be part of, or the property of.
(biology) The basic unit of a living organism, consisting of a quantity of protoplasm surrounded by a cell membrane, which is able to synthesize proteins and replicate itself. [from 19th c.]
Any enclosed space occupying or similar to a room.
The act or fact of communicating anything; transmission.
The central part of a fruit, containing the kernels or seeds.
An unstable situation, in political, social, economic or military affairs, especially one involving an impending abrupt change.

(logic) A process of reasoning that moves from the general to the specific, in which a conclusion follows necessarily from the premises presented, so that the conclusion cannot be false if the premises are true.
The act or process of dividing anything.
A person who drives a motorized vehicle such as a car, truck, bus, train, forklift, etc.
The occupation or work for which one is used, and often paid.
(transitive) To travel somewhere in search of discovery.
(mathematics) A relation in which each element of the domain is associated with exactly one element of the codomain.
(physics) A nuclear reaction in which nuclei combine to form more massive nuclei with the concomitant release of energy.
A tool with a heavy head and a handle used for pounding.
(transitive) To manage, control, or direct.
(transitive) To form a mental image of something; to envision or create something in one's mind.
Thousand-year-old; also (by extension, loosely) thousands of years old.
A self-contained component of a system, often interchangeable, which has a well-defined interface to the other components.
The residential area near one's home.
(formal, dated) Used to introduce a further negative statement.
(object-oriented programming) A feature pertaining to the dynamic treatment of data elements based on their type, allowing for a method to have several definitions.
Repetition of an activity to improve a skill.
The exclusive right of possessing, enjoying and disposing of a thing.
A female monarch.
Happening at constant (especially short) intervals. [from 17th c.]
(transitive) To stand or act in the place of; to perform the duties, exercise the rights, or otherwise act on behalf of
A second or subsequent reading of a text or artifact in an attempt to gain new insights.
(countable, politics) The popular removal and replacement of a government, especially by sudden violent action.
(anatomy) Any of a series of long curved bones occurring in 12 pairs in humans and other animals and extending from the spine to or toward the sternum.
Natural rubber or any of various synthetic materials with similar properties as natural rubber.
The act of saturating or the process of being saturated.
Free from the risk of financial loss; reliable.
(manner) In a serious or literal manner.
(uncountable, geology) A fine-grained homogeneous sedimentary rock composed of clay or volcanic ash which has been metamorphosed so that it cleaves easily into thin layers.

Specifically, a range of colours representing light (electromagnetic radiation) of contiguous frequencies; hence electromagnetic spectrum, visible spectrum, ultraviolet spectrum, etc. [from later 17th c.]

(anatomy, immunology) In vertebrates, including humans, a ductless vascular gland, located in the left upper abdomen near the stomach, which destroys old red blood cells, removes debris from the bloodstream, acts as a reservoir of blood, and produces lymphocytes.
Any of several carnivorous marine cephalopod mollusks, of the order Teuthida, having a mantle, eight arms, and a pair of tentacles
Conformity to fact or reality; correctness, accuracy.
The amount (of money or goods or services) that is considered to be a fair equivalent for something else.
Any tropical climbing orchid of the genus Vanilla (especially Vanilla planifolia), bearing podlike fruit yielding an extract used in flavoring food or in perfumes.
(mathematics) A quantity that may assume any one of a set of values.
Relating to or characteristic of life.
(relative) Used to refer to a previously mentioned person or people.
A given area distinguished on the basis of a particular characteristic, use, restriction, etc.
(intransitive) To relinquish or renounce a throne, or other high office or dignity; to renounce sovereignty. [First attested in the early 18th c.]
An institution for the study of higher learning; a college or a university; typically a private school. [First attested in the mid 16th century.]
An object of an ardent desire.
Ancient times; faraway history; former ages.

An enclosed area, often outdoor, for the presentation of sporting events (sports arena) or other spectacular events; earthen area, often oval, specifically for rodeos (North America) or circular area for bullfights (especially Hispanic America).
(physics, chemistry) Of or relating to atoms; composed of atoms; monatomic.
A person's romantic partner; a term of endearment used to refer to or address e.g. one's girlfriend, boyfriend, or spouse.
(British) A species in that genus, Abramis brama.
(of a colour) Both bright and saturated.
(colloquial, sometimes, derogatory) A homeless person, usually a man.

(business, organizational theory) A system of administration based upon organisation into bureaus, division of labour, a hierarchy of authority, etc., designed to dispose of a large body of work in a routine manner.
Balanced, rhythmic flow.
Relating to Cantonese language.
(pharmacy) A small container containing a dose of medicine.
A movie theatre, a movie house
(mineralogy) The tendency of a crystal to split along specific planes; schistosity. [from 19th c.]
A bunch or group of several discrete items that are close to each other.
A short textual designation, often with little relation to the item it represents.
A beverage made by infusing the beans of the coffee plant in hot water.
A fee charged by an agent or broker for carrying out a transaction.
The act or principle of compensating.
Not simple, easy, or straightforward; complicated.
An assemblage of related things; a collection.
A mate, companion, or associate. [from 1590s]
The act, process or ability of concentrating; the process of becoming concentrated, or the state of being concentrated.
A soft, fibrous, usually white substance consisting of fine hairs, especially the substance around the seeds of a plant of genus Gossypium.
To press between two hard objects; to squeeze so as to alter the natural shape or integrity, or to force together into a mass.
A solid composed of an array of atoms or molecules possessing long-range order and arranged in a pattern which is periodic in three dimensions.
(theology) An evil creature, the objectification of a hostile and destructive force.
(archaic) Excrement; dung.
A vessel such as a plate for holding or serving food, often flat with a depressed region in the middle.
(transitive) To terminate a union of multiple members actively, as by disbanding.
(transitive) To apportion (more or less evenly).

(mythology) Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.
A person at a newspaper, publisher or similar institution who edits stories and/or decides which ones to publish.
Operating on the physical behavior of electrons, especially in semiconductors.
A political unit ruled by an emperor or empress.
(thermodynamics, countable) A measure of the amount of energy in a physical system that cannot be used to do work.
A person who gave or supposedly gave their name to a people, place, institution, etc.
Destruction by abrasive action of fluids.
That which is excluded from others; a person, thing, or case, specified as distinct, or not included.
(usually, uncountable) A collection of events and/or activities from which an individual or group may gather knowledge, opinions, and skills.
A religious or spiritual belief system.
Destiny; often with a connotation of death, ruin, misfortune, etc.
Sensation, particularly through the skin.
Solenopsis spp. (tropical fire ants)
The side of something, in general senses.
Capable of adapting or changing to suit new or modified conditions or situations.
Originating from, characteristic of, belonging to, or being a citizen of a country or place other than the one under discussion.
A place for discussion.
A pathogen: a pathogenic microorganism, such as a bacterium or virus.
A mythical human of very great size.
(religion, especially, Eastern Christianity) A type of religious painting portraying a saint or scene from Scripture, often done on wooden panels.
A peculiarity that serves to distinguish or identify.
Naive; artless.
(music) The position of a chord which has a note other than the root as its bass note.
The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
A section of learning or teaching into which a wider learning content is divided.
Of or relating to lines.
(computing, of a resource) Connected directly to a particular computer, processor, etc.; able to be accessed offline.
A person who plays with or practices allegedly supernatural magic.

Any plant of the genus Mandragora, certain of which are said to have medicinal or aphrodisiac properties; the root of these plants often resembles the shape of a small person, hence occasioning various mythic, magical, or occult uses.
Any song in the genre of music written for marching (see Wikipedia's article on this type of music)
Relating to war.
A woman, specifically one with great control, authority or ownership.
Additional; further.
Normally associated with a particular person or thing; inherently related to the nature of a thing or creature. [from 14th c.]
Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.
(chemistry, physics) The massive, positively charged central part of an atom, made up of protons and neutrons.
(countable, medicine) A surgical procedure.
The beginning of something.
(Roman Catholicism, Anglican, Lutheranism, Eastern Orthodox) An administrative part of a diocese that has its own church.
(culinary) The edible seed of Pisum sativum.
The highest value reached by some quantity in a time period.
An academic discipline that seeks truth through reasoning rather than empiricism, often attempting to provide explanations relating to general concepts such as existence and rationality.
(UK, Australia, New Zealand) A single delivery of letters; the letters or deliveries that make up a single batch delivered to one person or one address. [from 17th c.]
(slang, derogatory) Someone (especially a male) who is unpleasant, rude or annoying. [from 16th c.]
An occupation, trade, craft, or activity in which one has a professed expertise in a particular area; a job, especially one requiring a high level of skill or training. [from 15th c.]
Standing out or raised; swollen.

The act (action) or process of punishing, imposing and/or applying a sanction, typically by an authority or a person in authority (for example: a parent or teacher), especially when disappointed or dissatisfied with the behavior or actions of a child, student, or someone else being looked after.
(transitive) To clean thoroughly; to rid of impurities; to cleanse.
A period, instance, or state of isolation from the general public or from native livestock and flora enacted to prevent the spread of any contagious disease.
The state of being actual or real; realness.
(uncountable, electronics) The act or ability to receive radio or similar signals.
(computing, not comparable) of a program or function that calls itself
(transitive) To bend back (light, etc.) from a surface.
(postpositive, followed by "for") Having the duty of taking care of something; answerable for an act performed or for its consequences; accountable; amenable, especially legally or politically.
The total income received from a given source.
(of fruits, vegetables, seeds etc.) Ready for reaping or gathering; having attained perfection; mature.
(ergative) To revolve by turning over and over; to move by turning on a horizontal axis; to impel forward with a revolving motion on a supporting surface.
Pertaining to an idealised form of love (originally, as might be felt by the heroes of a romance); conducive to romance; loving, affectionate. [from 18th c.]
Feeling sorrow; sorrowful, mournful.
Barbaric; not civilized.
Sexual activity; concern with, or interest in, sexual activity.
(transitive) To close (a building) so that no one can enter.
(pronunciation) Not pronounced; having no sound; quiescent.
(of color) Dull; not bright or colorful.
Dark; gloomy; shadowy, dimly lit.
One's male offspring.
A device through which a liquid is passed for purification, filtering or separation from solid matter; anything (including a screen or a cloth) used to strain a liquid.
Satisfying; having sufficient substance to be nourishing or filling.
A connected series or succession of objects; a number of things used or classed together. [from 16th c.]
Dreadful; causing terror, alarm and fear; awesome
(zoology) A projection or point in other parts of the body resembling the tooth of a vertebrate animal.
(uncountable, music genre) Ellipsis of trance music (“genre of electronic dance music”).

(mathematics) The replacement of the variables in an algebraic expression by their values in terms of another set of variables; a mapping of one space onto another or onto itself; a function that changes the position or direction of the axes of a coordinate system.
(geometry) An angular point of a polygon, polyhedron or higher order polytope.
(US, chiefly, New England) A shopping cart.
(countable, law) Someone called to give evidence in a court.
Of or relating to acid; having the character of an acid.
A curve, in general. [from 17th c.]
A sharp edge or ridge formed by the intersection of two surfaces
(intransitive) To wait; to stay in waiting.
(military) An army unit having two or more companies, etc. and a headquarters. Traditionally forming part of a regiment.
(in the plural) The entrails or intestines; the internal organs of the stomach.
Any major progress; such as a great innovation or discovery that overcomes a significant obstacle.
A hole through which a button is pushed to secure a garment or some part of one.
(intransitive) To give birth to a calf.
A division of Luxembourg, Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, etc.
Containing, composed of, or consisting of fat.
(ichthyology, zootomy) One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre/maneuver.
A part broken off; a small, detached portion; an imperfect part, either physically or not
A pile or mass; a collection of things laid in a body, or thrown together so as to form an elevation.
The impersonal pronoun, used without referent as the subject of an impersonal verb or statement (known as the dummy pronoun, dummy it or weather it).
A semiprecious stone, either nephrite or jadeite, generally green or white in color, often used for carving figurines.
A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
Pierce with or as if with a lance.
The part, or fate, that falls to one, as it were, by chance, or without one's planning.
(transitive) To attract by temptation, appeal, or guile.
Feeling or expressing sorrow over someone's death.
A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
(transitive) To don (clothing, equipment, or the like).
Rhyming: sameness of letters or sounds of part of some words.
(transitive) To move (one object) while maintaining contact with another object over some area, with pressure and friction.
The activity of running as a form of exercise, as a sport, or for any other reason.
A broad piece of defensive armor, held in hand, formerly in general use in war, for the protection of the body.
A quantity obtained by addition or aggregation.
Containing nothing; empty; not occupied or filled.
Yes.
Suitable or fit; proper; felicitous.
On the top of.
A small, round object.
To shout in a deep voice.
Very hot.
A flower, especially one indicating that a fruit tree is fruiting; (collectively) a mass of such flowers.
(transitive) To cause to leap.
That which holds anything tightly or supports it firmly; a bandage or a prop.
A domestic utensil with fibers bound together at the end of a long handle, used for sweeping.
A newly sprouted leaf or blossom that has not yet unfolded.
(intransitive) To grow or expand.
The slender, flexible main stem of a plant such as bamboo, including many species in the grass family Gramineae
Cold enough to cause discomfort.
A group of singers performing together; a choir; specifically, such a group singing together in a musical, an opera, etc., as distinct from the soloists; an ensemble.

A bivalve mollusk of many kinds, especially those that are edible; for example soft-shell clams (Mya arenaria), hard clams (Mercenaria mercenaria), sea clams or hen clams (Spisula solidissima), and other species, possibly originally applied to clams of species Tridacna gigas, a huge East Indian bivalve.
To scratch or to tear at.
A group of vehicles travelling together for safety, especially one with an escort.
A tuft, or other natural ornament, growing on an animal's head, for example the comb of a cockerel, the swelling on the head of a snake, the lengthened feathers of the crown or nape of bird, etc.
A rocky outcrop.
(figuratively) A bit, small amount.
A piece of cloth covering a window, bed, etc. to offer privacy and keep out light.
(intransitive) To have enough courage (to do something).
The number two.
(dice) A cast of dice totalling two.
A plan, drawing, sketch or outline to show how something works, or show the relationships between the parts of a whole.
A stain or spot (on clothes etc); any foreign substance that worsens appearance.
A specific branch of knowledge, learning, or practice.
(ambitransitive) To avoid (something) by moving suddenly out of the way.
(of a being) Lacking strength; weak; languid; inclined to lose consciousness
The pungent aromatic rhizome of a tropical Asian herb, Zingiber officinale, used as a spice and as a stimulant and acarminative.

resolutions.keep_english: The speaker or writer, referred to as the grammatical subject, of a sentence.
Having a fine edge or point; sharp.
(colloquial) Uncool, uninteresting, or unfunny.
A group or association of cooperating members.
(of a person or animal) Slim; not fleshy.
(archaic) The owner of a house, piece of land, or other possession

resolutions.word: may god have mercy on your soul

resolutions.keep_english: Used to introduce a sentence in order to draw attention to the importance of what is being said.
A step.
A manner of walking, running or dancing; the rate or style of how someone moves with their feet. [from 14th c.]
(transitive, vulgar) To sexually penetrate, typically in a vigorous manner.
(Internet) To publish (a message) to a newsgroup, forum, blog, etc. [from 20th c.]
(intransitive, or, with 'that' clause, or, 'to' infinitive) To speak or behave so as to give a false or simulated appearance. [from 14th c.]
A chain of mountains.
(transitive, or, intransitive) To hurry; to perform a task with great haste, often not properly or without thinking carefully.
A reddish-brown color.
A writing; a written document.
An ear of corn or grain.
(transitive) To take illegally, or without the owner's permission, something owned by someone else without intending to return it.
(cooking, uncountable, countable) Broth made from meat (originally bones) or vegetables, used as a basis for stew or soup.
In a limited manner; only
A small object that protrudes from something; an ornamental knob.
(intransitive) To be enough or sufficient; to meet the need (of anything); to be adequate; to be good enough.

A chemical element (symbol S) with an atomic number of 16, having a bright yellow color and characteristic smell, used commercially in a variety of products such as insecticides, black powder, and matchsticks.
A band of fabric worn around the wrist or head during sports to absorb perspiration.
A long bladed weapon with a grip and typically a pommel and crossguard (together forming a hilt), which is designed to cut, stab, slash and/or hack.
(Canada, US) A large spoon, used for eating food from a bowl.
To make use of something.
(transitive) To deal with, handle.
(idiomatic) To act on one's own authority, without asking for permission.
(idiomatic, intransitive, with "in") To participate or join.
The part of a sword blade to which the handle is fastened.
Any of several small turtles of the families Emydidae and Geoemydidae found throughout the world.
(anatomy) The region of the mammalian body between the neck and abdomen as well as the cavity containing the heart and lungs.
(outside, North America, uncountable) Wood that has been pre-cut and is ready for use in construction.
Indicating destination or final position: In the direction of, so as to arrive at or reach.
(literally) Relating to, or produced today.
(physiology) The process of giving off water vapour through the skin or mucous membranes.
(archaic) The action of trying (to do) something, especially more than once. (This sense is still current in the expression trial and error.)
The lining of the large stomach of ruminating animals, when prepared for food.
(transitive) To power up, to put into operation, to start, to activate (an appliance, light, mechanism, functionality etc.).
A circular rotating platform.
(added to adjectives or past participles) not
Going up a slope or a hill.
A vessel for the ashes or cremains of a deceased person.
Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of bears.
(gymnastics) A gymnastic movement performed on this apparatus.
(medicine) Of a disease or disease-causing agent: malignant, able to cause damage to the host.
(intransitive) To stop sleeping; to awake.
A moderate degree of heat; the sensation of being warm.
(transitive) To form something by passing lengths or strands of material over and under one another.
Health, safety, happiness and prosperity; well-being in any respect.
An injury to the upper spine connected to a violent jerk of the head in either a backward or forward or side to side direction, resembling the motion of a whip.
(North America, informal) The penis. [from 20th c.]
(ditransitive) To bestow (a thought or gesture) towards (someone or something).
(obsolete) A staff, rod, or stick.
Thus far; up to the present; up to some unspecified time.
To give as a result or outcome; to produce or render.

A bar or frame by which two oxen or other draught animals are joined at their necks enabling them to pull a cart, plough, etc.; (by extension) a device attached to a single draught animal for the same purpose.
